steps.title: Identify what "emphasize a similarity" requires
steps.content: The task asks for a sentence that highlights what the two positions have in common. The notes describe first position (Vaganova method) and demi-seconde position (Royal Academy of Dance) — both are arm positions. A sentence emphasizing a similarity must name both positions and state what they share, not contrast them or describe only one.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'First position in Vaganova method ballet and demi-seconde position in Royal Academy of Dance ballet are both positions for the dancer's arms.': Names both positions and states exactly what they share — they are both arm positions. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Both first position and demi-seconde position are positions in Royal Academy of Dance ballet.': Incorrectly attributes first position to the Royal Academy of Dance; first position belongs to Vaganova method. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In first position, both arms are rounded to roughly align the fingers with the navel, in contrast to demi-seconde position, where both arms are out to the sides but angled downward.': Explicitly contrasts the two positions rather than emphasizing a similarity. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In Vaganova method ballet, there are a number of positions, including first position.': Describes only Vaganova method and never mentions demi-seconde position at all, so no comparison is made. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
